# ERP Shell SvelteKit LLM Guide

Purpose: guide agents implementing or customizing the `erp-shell-sveltekit`
template — a single-company ERP shell on Cloudflare SvelteKit where operational
modules plug into a lock-driven left sidebar. See `CLAUDE.md` for the sidebar
mechanism and the module wiring map.

## Read First

- `microservices.template.json`
- `microservices.config.json`
- `microservices.lock.json`
- `README.agent.md`
- `docs/api-boundary.md`

## Modules

- `auth` — signing keys / token verification (session wiring point)
- `org-team-rbac` — organizations, memberships, roles, invitations, authorize
- `billing-subscriptions` — plans, subscriptions, status, dunning
- `admin-shell` — generic super-admin table gateway over module tables
- `audit-log` — append-only event trail

## Architecture Rule

SvelteKit route files are adapters. Domain/API logic lives in detached use cases.

```txt
src/routes/signup/+page.server.ts        -> createOrganization (org-team-rbac)
src/routes/app/+layout.server.ts         -> resolvePermissions gate (org-team-rbac)
src/routes/app/team/+page.server.ts      -> inviteMember / updateMemberRole
src/routes/app/team/accept/+page.server.ts -> acceptInvitation
src/routes/app/billing/+page.server.ts   -> listPlans / startSubscription / changePlan
src/routes/admin/[resource]/+page.server.ts -> listRecords (admin-shell)
src/routes/api/desktop/import/+server.ts -> authenticated approved-draft import adapter

src/lib/server/stores.ts   -> resolves D1 or memory module adapters per request
src/lib/server/org-context.ts -> active-org tracking + requireOrgPermission gate
```

Desktop import is not bidirectional database sync. The Tauri app keeps local
files, local draft JSON, review edits, and retry context. The Worker is the
canonical multi-user ERP backend: D1 for records, R2 for bytes, KV for shared
gateway state, Queues for async module jobs, and audit-log for traceability.

## Tenancy Rules

- Each org is a tenant. `subscriberId` on a subscription is the org id.
- `resolvePermissions(orgId, userId)` returns `[]` for non-members.
- Gate every org action with `authorize(orgId, userId, permission, { store })`.
- The `/admin` area is platform super-admin scope (across all orgs), gated by the
  session `isSuperAdmin` flag, not an org role.

## Approval Gates

Require user approval before changing:

- secrets
- remote migrations
- payment/email provider behavior (Stripe, etc.)
- Cloudflare resources
- remote deployments
- forked module internals

Local baseline commands may apply the checked-in D1 migrations to Wrangler's local
database:

```bash
pnpm microservices local migrate
pnpm microservices local dev
pnpm microservices local smoke
```
